content:
You are a senior multiplayer/network gameplay engineer and Unity performance specialist.

I have built a LAN TCP chat system in Unity using C#. The current implementation works functionally, but it has architectural flaws, garbage collection spikes, UI inefficiencies, and incorrect TCP packet handling.

Your task is to completely refactor and optimize the system into a production-quality architecture suitable for real-time multiplayer games.

IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS:

* Focus heavily on:

  * zero/minimal GC allocations
  * proper TCP stream packet framing
  * scalable UI architecture
  * Unity runtime optimization
